exercises.title: Personal Introduction
exercises.description: Write a program that prints three lines: your name (use 'Alice'), your city ('Paris'), and one thing you want to learn ('I want to learn Python!').
exercises.starterCode:
# TODO: Print your name on one line
# TODO: Print your city on one line
# TODO: Print what you want to learn on one line
exercises.expectedOutput:
Alice
Paris
I want to learn Python!
hints: You need three print() calls, one per line of output.
Each string must be in quotes: print("Alice")

exercises.title: Business Card
exercises.description: Create a formatted business card using print(). Output a header line of 30 equal signs, then 'Name: Alice', then 'Role: Python Student', then another 30 equal signs.
exercises.starterCode:
# TODO: Print a line of 30 equal signs using string repetition
# TODO: Print 'Name: Alice'
# TODO: Print 'Role: Python Student'
# TODO: Print another line of 30 equal signs
hints: Use string repetition: print("=" * 30) prints 30 equal signs.

exercises.title: Separator Lines and Blank Lines
exercises.description: Practice controlling output layout. Print 'Section A', then a blank line, then 'Section B', then a blank line, then 'Section C'.
exercises.starterCode:
# TODO: Print 'Section A'
# TODO: Print a blank line (empty print)
# TODO: Print 'Section B'
# TODO: Print a blank line
# TODO: Print 'Section C'
exercises.expectedOutput:
Section A

Section B

Section C
hints: An empty print() call produces a blank line.
print() with no arguments outputs a newline character only.

warmup.title: Quick Recall: Strings and Quotes
warmup.description: Strings must be in quotes. What does this program print?
warmup.starterCode:
# Predict the output before running
print("Hello,", "World", "Python")
print("Two plus two equals", 2 + 2)
warmup.expectedOutput:
Hello, World Python
Two plus two equals 4
